mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and stable consumer price indices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of periods following industrial production acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 250.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 250.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal rising construction activity and consumer confidence in long-term economic stability, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for housing starts against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 2 percent indicates a sudden contraction in consumer spending that immediately pressures corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of instances following a retail sales drop exceeding 2 percent.
